title: Personal Information
shortSummary: Share your name, nationality, age, and job, and ask others about themselves in polite Korean.
culturalNote: When Koreans introduce themselves they usually give their name with the family name first, so a person named Kim Minsu is addressed by the family name Kim. Age comes up early in Korean conversation, not as nosiness but because it decides which politeness level and which forms of address each person should use. Asking 몇 살이에요? (myeot sarieyo, how old are you?) is normal between new acquaintances of similar standing. Job and company are also common opening topics, and people often exchange business cards (명함, myeongham) with two hands as a sign of respect. Until you know someone's age and position you stay in the polite -요 form; once relationships are clear, an older or senior person may invite you to speak more casually.

grammar.explanation: To mark the topic of a sentence (often the subject) Korean adds 은 after a word ending in a consonant and 는 after a vowel. 저 (jeo, I) becomes 저는 (jeoneun). This particle introduces what you are talking about.
grammar.commonMistake: Using 는 after a consonant; words like 이름 (ireum) take 은, giving 이름은.

grammar.explanation: To say 'A is B' Korean attaches 이에요 after a consonant and 예요 after a vowel to the noun. 학생 (haksaeng, student) becomes 학생이에요, while 민수 (Minsu) becomes 민수예요.
grammar.commonMistake: Using 예요 after a consonant; after a consonant you need 이에요.

dialogue.setup: Two students, Minsu and Sarah, meet at a language exchange and introduce themselves.

lines.speaker: 사라
lines.english: My name is Sarah. Where are you from?

lines.speaker: 민수
lines.english: I'm from Seoul. And you, Sarah?

lines.speaker: 사라
lines.english: I'm from Australia. What is your job?

lines.speaker: 민수
lines.english: I'm an office worker. And you, Sarah?

lines.speaker: 사라
lines.english: I'm a student. I speak a little Korean.

lines.speaker: 민수
lines.english: You speak well! Nice to meet you.

quiz.question: How do you ask 'What is your name?' in Korean?
quiz.explanation: 이름이 뭐예요? (ireumi mwoyeyo) literally asks 'what is the name?'

quiz.question: Which sentence correctly says 'I'm a student'?
quiz.explanation: 학생 ends in a consonant, so it takes 이에요, giving 학생이에요.

quiz.question: 저___ 호주에서 왔어요. (topic particle after a vowel)
quiz.explanation: 저 (jeo) ends in a vowel, so the topic particle is 는, giving 저는.

quiz.question: What does 몇 살이에요? ask?
quiz.explanation: 몇 살이에요? (myeot sarieyo) asks someone's age, a common early question in Korea.

quiz.question: Arrange: 왔어요 / 저는 / 호주에서 (I'm from Australia)
quiz.explanation: Korean order is topic + place + verb: 저는 호주에서 왔어요.
